settings.title: Ten Years of Front Doors Left Unlocked
settings.excerpt: A decade of guests later, the visitors' book has become the thing we would save first in a fire.
settings.body:
This spring marks ten years since we lit the first fire at Hearthstone and waited, nervously, for our first guests to arrive.

We read back through the visitors' books to mark it. Proposals, anniversaries, a few names that now return every single year, and one couple who first stayed on their honeymoon and came back this winter with two children.

Ten years of bringing the house back

When we took the place on, it was a cold and tired old house with good bones and not much else. The first decade has been a long, happy job of warming it back up, one room and one fireplace at a time. The visitors' books have filled up alongside the work, and reading them back is the best record we have of those years:

  • Proposals planned in nervous handwriting.
  • Anniversaries marked year after year by the same couples.
  • Children of guests who first came on their own honeymoons.

To say thank you, we are hosting a free afternoon tea this summer for the neighbours and tradespeople who helped us bring the old house back to life.

The people who helped

We did not do any of it alone. The local builders, the plumber who knows every quirk of the pipes, and the neighbours who lent a hand and a kind word all have a place in the story. The afternoon tea is a small way of saying so.

Ten years on, the kettle is always on and the door is rarely locked. Thank you for filling the place with stories.
